title: "Binder Error: Referenced table not found!" occurred in the HAVING clause of subquery
description:
### What happens?

The following query triggers an unexpected error: `Binder Error: Referenced table "t0" not found!`

```
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 REAL, c1 REAL);
SELECT (SELECT t0.c0 FROM t0 WHERE 1 GROUP BY t0.c0 HAVING t0.c1);
```

### To Reproduce

I download the binary file of DuckDB from https://github.com/duckdb/duckdb/releases/download/v1.1.3/duckdb_cli-linux-amd64.zip and run it in Ubuntu 22.04

### OS:

Ubuntu

### DuckDB Version:

1.1.3

### DuckDB Client:

command line

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Chi Zhang

### Affiliation:

Nanjing University

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a stable release

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, value VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE t2(a FLOAT, b INTEGER DEFAULT 0);
SELECT (SELECT t1.value FROM t1 WHERE t1.id > 1 GROUP BY t1.value HAVING COUNT(t2.b) > 0);
SELECT (SELECT t2.a FROM t2 WHERE t2.a IS NULL GROUP BY t2.a HAVING COUNT(t2.b) > 0);
key_bug_features: HAVING clause in subquery with missing referenced table
